title: Eduversal AI Competency Framework
subtitle: Network-wide AI competency, classroom guidance, and institutional maturity model for Eduversal partner schools

_meta.documentRole: Single canonical spine for the Eduversal AI Competency Framework. Indexes both REFERENCE-layer documents (verbatim authoritative sources — frozen, citable) and PRACTICAL-layer documents (classroom-facing playbooks, prompt libraries, decision trees — designed for daily use). Every practical block carries a referenceAnchor pointing back to a reference block, so the question 'why are we doing this?' always resolves to a citable source. This file is the only file that other code (build.js, reader pages, chip wiring, Firestore rules) needs to start from.

twoLayerModel.summary: The framework is split into a frozen REFERENCE layer (authoritative source documents — verbatim, citable, slow-changing) and a living PRACTICAL layer (role-specific playbooks, prompt libraries, classroom decision trees, weekly tips — fast-changing, sahaya yönelik). Educators use the practical layer day-to-day; the reference layer answers 'why does this guidance exist?' via a one-click chip popover.
twoLayerModel.rationale: Educator-facing tools must not drown teachers in framework prose. Verbatim source text is non-negotiable for audit and provenance (Common Mistake #9 — never inline verbatim into derivative content), but verbatim is not what a teacher needs at 7am Monday before a Year 8 lesson. The two-layer model lets us keep both invariants intact: full provenance AND practical utility.

doNotFabricate.rule: No practical guidance, prompt template, or workflow may be added to a playbook unless it is anchored to (a) a verbatim block in the reference layer, OR (b) a confirmed external source listed in externalAnchors with a working URL, OR (c) explicitly flagged as 'practitioner-pattern' with author attribution and a date. Inventing a 'best practice' without provenance is the single most common failure mode in AI-guide writing and is explicitly forbidden here.
doNotFabricate.enforcement: Every task / prompt / workflow object in practical/*.json carries a required 'provenance' field. Empty provenance = lint failure at build time.

indonesiaFirst.summary: Practical guidance is calibrated for Indonesian Cambridge partner schools. Bahasa Indonesia LLM-quality notes, UU PDP No. 27/2022 data-protection context, Permendikdasmen 13/2025 Koding & AI elective curriculum integration, and the local reality that no Permendikdasmen AI-USE (teacher conduct) regulation exists yet are first-class concerns, not appendices. Western-default examples are explicitly localised where the underlying advice changes (e.g., parent communication tone, classroom AI-disclosure norms, exam authenticity expectations under Cambridge International).

rolesNotHubs.summary: Earlier drafts split practical content by hub (TH/AH/CH). The Eduversal v1.0 framework instead splits by AUDIENCE (Teacher / Student-via-teacher / Institutional Leader). This file adopts the v1.0 split. A subject_teacher who also sits on an AH-side committee reads BOTH the teacher-playbook AND the leader-playbook — not a TH-specific mishmash.

primarySource.title: AI Competency Framework — For Eduversal Partner Schools (v1.0)
primarySource.note: Verbatim text mirrored into reference/eduversal-v1-part1-teacher.json, reference/eduversal-v1-part2-student.json, reference/eduversal-v1-part3-institutional.json — to be authored after this manifest is approved.

supportingResearchBriefing.scope: Authoritative 2025-2026 AI-in-education sources cited in externalAnchors below
supportingResearchBriefing.note: Compiled by background research agent; all URLs verified at time of compilation. URL rot is expected — flag broken links during annual review.

cambridgeInternational.title: Cambridge International — Teaching, learning, assessment and AI

curriculumRefresh2026.title: Cambridge drives a new era of digital maturity for the age of AI
curriculumRefresh2026.scope: Primary + Lower Secondary Digital Literacy curriculum refresh

keyDocuments: The use of generative AI in coursework (Exams Officers Guide Phase 3)
Getting started with AI in the classroom
AI and the environment — guidance for schools
cambridgeInternational.usage: Cambridge is the curriculum authority for all Eduversal partner schools. Any AI-in-coursework decision, plagiarism judgement, or exams-officer process must defer to the live Cambridge hub above. Cambridge does NOT publish a single standalone 'AI policy for schools' PDF — guidance is dispersed across the hub. Cite the hub plus the specific document title.

permendikdasmen13_2025.title: Permendikdasmen No. 13 Tahun 2025 — Koding & AI in Kurikulum 2025/2026
permendikdasmen13_2025.scope: Curriculum scope only (elective subjects: SD kelas 5, SMP kelas 7, SMA/SMK kelas 10; phased, school self-assesses readiness; NOT compulsory).
permendikdasmen13_2025.boundary: This regulation is about WHAT students learn ABOUT AI, NOT about HOW teachers USE AI. No Permendikdasmen AI-USE (teacher conduct) regulation exists as of 2026-05-16. The Eduversal AI Competency Framework v1.0 explicitly fills this regulatory gap for the Eduversal network.

uuPdp27_2022.title: UU Perlindungan Data Pribadi No. 27 Tahun 2022
uuPdp27_2022.dataResidency: No hard localisation mandate, but cross-border transfer burden of proof is high. For student PII the conservative posture is in-country processing or vendor contractual guarantees.
uuPdp27_2022.practicalImplication: Teachers entering identifiable student data into public AI tools (ChatGPT free tier, free Claude, etc.) is a UU PDP compliance risk for the school, not just a privacy preference. This must be in every teacher-playbook anti-pattern list.

indonesiaRegulation.note: PIGP chip family currently scopes Permendiknas 27/2010 (induction). UU PDP and Permendikdasmen 13/2025 are different regulatory instruments — consider whether they need their own chip family or extend the existing one. Decision deferred to Phase 1b chip-wiring task.

unescoAiCft.title: UNESCO AI competency framework for teachers

mappingToEduversalV1._note: Eduversal v1.0 uses Foundation/Practitioner/Leader × Domain A/B/C/D/E. The mapping below lets a reader-page chip popover show 'this Eduversal Foundation-level Domain A descriptor corresponds to UNESCO's Acquire-level AI foundations and applications aspect'. Mapping is best-fit, not 1:1 — Eduversal v1.0 explicitly does not mirror UNESCO.

anthropic.signaturePractice: Learning mode (Socratic guiding, not direct answering)
anthropic.bahasaIndonesiaQuality: Leading — natural, idiomatic per Artificial Analysis Multilingual Index

openai.keyPolicy: Not trained on educator data by default; SAML SSO; FERPA-aware; free to US K-12 through June 2027
openai.bahasaIndonesiaQuality: Correct but slightly formal per practitioner reads

google.keyPolicy: Operates as 'school official' under FERPA; COPPA-aware; no training on Workspace-for-Education data; admin Vault search + usage reporting

oecd.title: What should teachers teach and students learn in a future of powerful AI?

oecdEcAiLiteracyFramework.title: AI Literacy Framework for Primary & Secondary Education

wef.title: Why AI Literacy is Now a Core Competency in Education + AILit Framework

iste.approach: Incremental ISTE Standards refresh embedding AI; digital citizenship strand carries heaviest changes; no standalone AI framework

practitionerPatterns2026._note: Concrete patterns being shared by practising teachers in 2025-2026. Cited so that practical/*.json playbooks can reference WORKING patterns instead of invented advice.

megaPrompt4Part.usage: Reused for quizzes, rubrics, report-card comments, IEP goals

stepwisePblScaffold.pattern: Step-wise PBL/unit scaffolding instead of one-shot prompts

differentiationIsTopUseCase.stat: ~61% of teacher AI use is differentiation
differentiationIsTopUseCase.rubricAnchoredFeedback: Rubric-anchored feedback prompts outperform open prompts

toolRecommendations._purpose: Canonical list of AI tools recommended for educator use across the Eduversal network. All playbooks, prompts, and decision trees reference this list. Drift is avoided by reading from here, not from inline mentions elsewhere.

_meta.sourceBriefing: Background research agent scan, May 2026. All tools verified active at time of compilation; URL rot expected — annual review required.
_meta.writingStyle: Plain English. Tool descriptions ≤ 2 sentences. Each tool tagged with what it shines at, not marketing language.
_meta.approvalStatus: Eduversal Academic Directorate has not yet published a network-approved tool list for 2026-2027. The 'coreThreeProbable' tools below are the most likely network-approved candidates based on (a) educator tier availability with proper data-handling contracts, (b) Bahasa Indonesia language quality, (c) UU PDP compliance posture, (d) ubiquity among Cambridge International schools in 2025-2026. Each playbook references this section pending formal approval.
_meta.annualReview: Term 3 each year. Add new tools, retire discontinued ones, update Bahasa quality notes.

coreThreeProbable._note: These three are the most likely Eduversal network-approved tools for 2026-2027. All offer school-tier products with data-handling contracts that exclude school data from model training. Final approval pending Academic Directorate.

items.name: Claude for Education (Anthropic)
items.shinesAt: Long-document drafting, curriculum work, Artifacts feature for interactive resources, careful tone on cultural-bias and ethics questions
items.evidenceOfFit: Anthropic Education Report 2025: 57% of educator conversations are curriculum work. Teach For All partnership covers 63 countries / 100k+ educators including Indonesia.
items.limitations: Slower than competitors at fast-iteration tasks. US-hosted; verify DPA terms before processing identifiable student work.
items.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strongest fit for HQ Subject Specialists drafting curriculum, and for school leaders drafting policy. Most cautious on cultural-bias rephrasing — better for materials going network-wide where errors matter.

items.name: ChatGPT for Teachers (OpenAI)
items.shinesAt: General drafting, summarisation, creative ideation, fast iteration
items.evidenceOfFit: OpenAI launched ChatGPT for Teachers Nov 2025 — free to US K-12 through June 2027. SAML SSO. Not trained on educator data by default. FERPA-aware.
items.limitations: Bahasa output tends slightly more formal than typical Indonesian classroom register. Read aloud before sending to parents.
items.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strongest fit for classroom teachers needing fast iteration across many small tasks. Easiest learning curve for first-time AI users.

items.name: Gemini for Education (Google)
items.shinesAt: Workspace integration (Docs, Slides, Classroom), NotebookLM-style document grounding, classroom workflow embedding
items.evidenceOfFit: Operates as 'school official' under FERPA. COPPA-aware. No training on Workspace-for-Education data. Free Gemini training rolled out to 6M US educators via ISTE+ASCD partnership 2026.
items.limitations: Best fit only if school is already on Google Workspace for Education. Cross-tool workflow needed otherwise.
items.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strongest fit for schools already deeply using Google Workspace. Best Bahasa Indonesia quality of the three per 2025-2026 Artificial Analysis Multilingual Index.

lessonPlanningAndDrafting.categoryNote: General-purpose drafting work (lesson plans, units, rubrics, communications). The 'core three' tools above are the primary picks here.

tools.name: Claude for Education
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Long lesson plans where careful sequencing matters. Subject Leader curriculum work.

tools.name: ChatGPT for Teachers
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Fast iteration. Brainstorming many starter activities or task variations quickly.

tools.name: Gemini for Education
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Lesson planning where output flows straight into Google Docs / Slides / Classroom.

tools.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
tools.shinesAt: Drafting inside Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Teams where staff already work
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strong only if school is M365-based. Otherwise duplicates Gemini's role.

differentiationAndWorksheets.categoryNote: Tools specifically built for making differentiated practice, scaffolded materials, leveled texts. The #1 teacher AI use case per Anthropic's 2025 report.

tools.name: MagicSchool AI
tools.tier: free-teacher / paid school district (SOC 2, FERPA, COPPA compliant)
tools.shinesAt: 80+ teacher tools in one place. Leveled text rewriter is the standout. IEP draft tool, scaffolded text, differentiated worksheets.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: If your school approves only one specialised tool beyond the core-three, MagicSchool's school tier is the strongest single addition. One-stop for the differentiation workflows that take most teacher time.

tools.name: Diffit
tools.shinesAt: Take any source text or URL and instantly generate leveled reading versions with question sets
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Single-purpose but very good at that purpose. Best for English, humanities, science teachers who work from textbook passages.

tools.name: Brisk Teaching
tools.tier: free / Premium / Schools (SOC 2, FERPA, COPPA, ISTE Seal)
tools.shinesAt: Chrome extension that lives inside Google Docs / Slides / YouTube. Adjusts reading level inline without leaving the document.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strong only for Google Workspace schools. The 'no context switch' workflow saves real time. 93% on Common Sense Privacy.

rubricAndFeedback.categoryNote: Tools for generating rubrics, comment banks, formative feedback drafts. Always remember: AI drafts, you mark.

tools.name: MagicSchool — Rubric Generator + Report Card Comments + Text Feedback
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Bundled in the same tool teachers use for differentiation — one login, less context switching.

tools.name: Brisk Teaching — inline feedback on student Docs
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Highlights and comments inside Google Docs where students wrote. Faster than copy-paste workflows.

tools.name: Eduaide.ai
tools.shinesAt: Teacher-only access (simpler privacy footprint). Feedback bot + 100+ resource templates.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Lower privacy risk because students never log in. Strong for solo teachers building feedback templates from rubrics.

rubricAndFeedback.criticalNote: Anthropic's 2025 educator report flagged that 48.9% of grading conversations fully delegated marking to AI. OECD 2025 explicitly insists on 'human in the loop' for assessment. This is the red line — see redFlagsAndRedlines.json#rl_02.

assessmentItemDrafting.categoryNote: MCQ generation, question banks, item drafting. Critical for HQ Subject Specialists. Remember the assessment boundary — AI drafts; humans author final-form formal items.

tools.name: Claude for Education / ChatGPT for Teachers
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Best for nuanced Cambridge-aligned items when prompted with the syllabus and command words. Better than dedicated quiz tools for Stage 7-9 Checkpoint-style stems.

tools.name: Wayground (formerly Quizizz) AI
tools.rebrandNote: Rebranded from Quizizz in 2025. Older bookmarks redirect.
tools.shinesAt: Bulk MCQ generation from documents/topics with distractors and gamified delivery
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strong for practice-bank seeding (NOT formal assessment). Pair with HQ specialist review.

tools.name: Questgen
tools.shinesAt: Bloom-level-tagged MCQ / true-false / short-answer generation from passages
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Useful for item-bank seeding from textbook chapters. Always review for distractor quality and cultural bias.

lessonResourceCreation.categoryNote: Slides, visuals, posters, infographics. Tools that turn text into classroom-ready materials.

tools.name: Canva for Education + Magic Studio
tools.shinesAt: Posters, slides, infographics. 2026 additions: Magic Layers, Learn Grid, Magic Activities, AI quiz generator.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Safest school-tier visual creation tool. Verified K-12 teacher accounts get full feature set free.

tools.name: Adobe Express for Education
tools.shinesAt: Firefly-powered generative fill. Commercially safe outputs (important if materials may be published).
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Alternative to Canva. Pick one — don't run both.

tools.name: Gamma
tools.shinesAt: Text-to-slide-deck. Fast first-draft decks from outlines.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Use for teacher-only deck drafting. Always review for accuracy before classroom use.

tools.name: NotebookLM (Google)
tools.shinesAt: Audio overviews and study guides from uploaded source documents. Turns curriculum docs into student-facing podcasts.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strong for Cambridge syllabus or Eduversal handbook analysis. School-tier through Workspace gives proper data handling.

aiDetectionAndAcademicIntegrity.categoryNote: CRITICAL: 2025-2026 consensus is that AI detection tools are unreliable, especially for EAL (second-language English) writers. A 2023 Patterns study found 7 detectors flagged 61% of non-native English essays as AI-generated. Turnitin AI detection has ~30% higher false-positive rate for ESL writers. Vanderbilt, Johns Hopkins, Northwestern, UCLA, UC San Diego, Yale, Waterloo, Oregon State all disabled Turnitin AI detection by late 2025.
aiDetectionAndAcademicIntegrity.eduversalGuidance: Do NOT use AI detection tools as evidence in academic integrity decisions for Eduversal students. They are second-language English writers. Detection false-positive rates make these tools unsafe. Use process-based integrity instead.

tools.name: Turnitin AI detection
tools.eduversalGuidance: Multiple universities disabled this 2024-2025 due to EAL false positives. Eduversal recommendation: do NOT use scores as evidence.

tools.name: GPTZero
tools.eduversalGuidance: Same issue as Turnitin. Detector scores are not evidence.

patterns: Google Docs version history — see how the work was written, not whether AI was used
Oral defence — ask the student to explain their work in conversation
Draft-stage check-ins — assess process not product
Task design that's hard to AI-cheat — in-class drafting, personalised topics, mid-process visibility
betterAlternatives.authoritativeRecommendation: Anthropic + OECD 2025-2026 both recommend redesigning assignments over relying on detection.

translationAndLanguageSupport.categoryNote: Parent communication, EAL support, multilingual classrooms.

tools.name: DeepL
tools.bahasaSupport: supported since May 2022; better register than Google Translate per Indonesian academic studies
tools.shinesAt: Natural-sounding parent letters EN ↔ ID. Tone-aware in ways Google Translate isn't.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Best single translation choice for parent communication. Use Pro tier if your school is sending volume.

tools.name: Google Translate
tools.shinesAt: Image translation, voice translation, mobile field use. Quick checks.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Acceptable for short non-parent-communication use. For parent letters, DeepL or Claude/ChatGPT give better register.

tools.name: Claude / ChatGPT for tone-aware translation
tools.shinesAt: Translation with context: 'translate this parent letter to Bahasa Indonesia, formal-but-warm register, addressing parents of a Year 8 student about a concern.' Better than translator tools because tone is controllable.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: For high-stakes parent communication (concerns, policy letters, formal announcements), use the core-three tools with explicit tone instructions, not raw translation tools.

studentFacingSafeAi.categoryNote: Tools designed for STUDENT use with safety guardrails, age-appropriate moderation, and teacher monitoring. NOT general-public tools given to students.

tools.name: SchoolAI
tools.shinesAt: Teacher dashboard shows every student's chat in real time. Intelligent grouping suggestions. Socratic tutoring style.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strongest single choice for supervised classroom AI use with students. Teacher monitoring is built in, not bolted on.

tools.name: Khanmigo (Khan Academy)
tools.shinesAt: Math tutoring tied to Khan content. Teacher chat-history dashboard. Moderation flag emails.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Strong for maths specifically where Khan content is the spine. International pricing varies — verify cost before recommending school-wide.

tools.name: Curipod
tools.shinesAt: Teacher-controlled — students NEVER prompt AI directly. Live polls and reflection prompts.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Most conservative safe-AI design. Best for younger years and first introduction to student-facing AI activities.

studentFacingSafeAi.authoritativeRecommendation: OECD 2025 specifically endorses tools 'co-created with teachers' and tutors that 'ask questions instead of delivering answers' — SchoolAI, Khanmigo, and Curipod all fit this pattern.

administrativeAndSchoolOperations.categoryNote: Meeting summarisation, document analysis, calendar/scheduling. Used most by school leaders and HQ.

tools.name: Otter.ai
tools.shinesAt: Live meeting transcription with summaries. Time-stamped highlights.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Acceptable for general staff meetings. Do NOT use for staff appraisal meetings, student safeguarding meetings, or any confidential discussion without enterprise-tier DPA.

tools.name: Fireflies.ai
tools.shinesAt: Stronger admin controls and compliance features than Otter for large deployments. Action-item extraction is strong.
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Alternative to Otter. Pick one. Fireflies tends to be better for institutions with compliance overhead.

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Multi-document policy bundle analysis — Cambridge handbooks, Permendiknas regulations, Eduversal framework documents. Read fast by uploading all to one notebook.

tools.name: Claude / ChatGPT (paid tiers only — never free for confidential docs)
tools.shinesAtForThisCategory: Long-document analysis when you need conversation rather than just summary.

administrativeAndSchoolOperations.uuPdpNote: All major tools listed are US/EU-hosted. For board minutes, HR documents, student records — use enterprise tiers with signed DPAs. Free tiers are unsafe for confidential institutional content.

indonesiaSpecific.categoryNote: Local AI tools serving Indonesian education. Honest assessment: thin options for teacher-productivity. Educators will rely primarily on Western tools.

tools.name: Ruangguru
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Student-facing learning platform, NOT a teacher productivity tool. May be useful for students supplementing classroom work; not a replacement for the educator AI tools above.

tools.name: Pijar Mahir (Telkom)
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Same caveat as Ruangguru — student-facing, not teacher productivity.

tools.name: Zenius
tools.note: Closed January 2023, partial reopening under new ownership reported. Verify current status before any recommendation.

tools.name: Merdeka Mengajar / Rumah Belajar (Kemendikbud)
tools.aiFeatures: AI-flavoured recommendations but no generative AI suite comparable to MagicSchool
tools.eduversalRecommendationContext: Useful for Kurikulum-aligned content reference. Not a teacher AI productivity tool.

indonesiaSpecific.honestSituation: Eduversal educators will rely on Western tools for AI productivity. The mitigation is: (a) school-tier / enterprise contracts with documented DPAs, (b) never paste student PII into free tiers, (c) train teachers on UU PDP minimum-data principle, (d) prefer Bahasa-aware tools (Claude, Gemini, DeepL) for parent communication.

uuPdpComplianceSummary.headline: Indonesia's UU PDP No. 27/2022 is fully enforceable since 17 October 2024. PDP Agency targeted operational 2026.
guidance: Free / personal tiers: acceptable for teacher-only drafting with NO student PII.
School / education / enterprise tiers: required for any sustained use that touches student work, even anonymised.
Cross-border data transfer is allowed but burden of proof is on the school. Document the DPA.
Annual approved-tools register review is now a regulatory expectation, not just good practice.

knownGaps._note: Things we looked for in 2025-2026 sources and did NOT find. Documented so that future authors don't waste time re-searching, and so we know which areas need original Eduversal authorship.
knownGaps.cambridgeStandaloneAiPolicy: No standalone Cambridge International 'AI policy for schools' PDF found. Guidance is dispersed across the Cambridge AI hub + Exams Officers Guide. Eduversal must synthesise practical guidance from dispersed sources.
knownGaps.indonesianAiTeacherConductRegulation: No Permendikdasmen AI-USE (teacher conduct) regulation found as of 2026-05-16. Permendikdasmen 13/2025 is curriculum-scope only. The Eduversal AI Competency Framework v1.0 fills this regulatory gap for the Eduversal network.
knownGaps.indonesianClassroomCulturalBiasStudy: No 2025-2026 study found on cultural-relevance bias in Indonesian classrooms when using Western-trained LLMs. Practical guidance in this area is currently practitioner-pattern, not research-backed — flag in playbooks accordingly.

chipFamily.wiring: shared-design/cambridge-crossref.js (auto-synced to 3 hub copies + Students Hub via build pipeline). Adds 5th chip family alongside CTS / SKL / PIGP / ES. Popover renders title + verbatim summary from reference/*.json + optional UNESCO mapping note + 'Open full reference →' deep link to /references?doc=eduversal-aicf-{part}.

filesystemLayout._note: Authored files relative to this manifest's location: docs/research/eduversal/ai-competency-framework/

reference._purpose: Frozen authoritative source documents. Verbatim text, citable, slow-changing. Authored AFTER practical layer drafts are reviewed — practical layer drives the authoring priority.
reference.eduversal-v1-part1-teacher.json: Verbatim Part 1 (Teacher AI Competency: Foundation/Practitioner/Leader × Domains A-E) from Eduversal v1.0 PDF
reference.eduversal-v1-part2-student.json: Verbatim Part 2 (Student AI Competency: Primary/Lower Secondary/Upper Secondary × Strands 1-4)
reference.eduversal-v1-part3-institutional.json: Verbatim Part 3 (Institutional AI Maturity: 5 levels × 6 domains)
reference.eduversal-v1-appendices.json: Verbatim Appendices A-D (self-assessment tools, observation guide, student literacy assessment examples, institutional summary template)
reference.unesco-ai-cft-2024.json: Selected verbatim blocks from UNESCO AI CFT 2024 (key principles + 15-block matrix summary + ICT CFT alignment notes). Not the full 52-page PDF — selected for what feeds chip popovers.
reference.external-sources-index.json: Indexed metadata for all externalAnchors URLs (title + URL + accessDate + 1-paragraph summary). For the references hub External Frameworks facet.

practical._purpose: Living, role-specific, classroom-facing playbooks. Authored FIRST (before reference verbatim) because they are what educators actually open day-to-day.
practical.teacher-playbook.json: Subject teachers + subject leaders. Daily/weekly workflows · subject-specific patterns · classroom scenarios · quick-wins · red flags · decision trees · paired with prompt-library.json.
practical.leader-playbook.json: AH leadership sub-roles (principal, foundation rep, academic coordinator, Cambridge coordinator). Strategic workflows · policy decisions · staff support · stakeholder communication · paired with the Institutional Maturity model (Part 3).
practical.specialist-playbook.json: CH HQ subject specialists (director, coordinator). Curriculum authoring with AI · question-bank workflows · item-bank quality control · AI item-generation boundaries (NEVER feeding chapter_mastery / ease_growth, per Common Mistake #33 + #38).
practical.prompt-library.json: Cross-role kopyala-yapıştır prompt templates organised by task category (lesson planning · differentiation · rubric drafting · feedback · parent communication · meeting summarisation · curriculum analysis · question generation). Each prompt anchored to a practitioner pattern in externalAnchors.practitionerPatterns2026.
practical.classroom-activities.json: Teacher-facing classroom activities to develop STUDENT AI literacy (mapped to Part 2 strands). E.g. 'Year 8 English: critically evaluate an AI-generated essay' — full lesson outline + materials + extension.
practical.decision-trees.json: Flowchart-style decision aids: 'Should I use AI for this task?' · 'A student submitted suspected AI work — what now?' · 'A parent asks why we use AI — how do I respond?' · 'My school has not adopted Tool X — can I use my personal account?'
practical.weekly-tips.json: Rotating 52-week tip schedule (Jakarta academic year aligned). Each tip = 1 paragraph + 1 concrete action + 1 reference anchor. Surfaces in hub dashboards via weekly-tip card.
practical.redFlagsAndRedlines.json: Network-wide UNCONDITIONAL anti-patterns. Crisp, unambiguous, NO-context-shifts-allowed list (entering student PII in public AI · using AI to grade summative assessments · using AI to author chapter tests · presenting AI output to parents as your own judgement · etc.). Each redline anchored to a reference (UU PDP, Eduversal v1.0 Domain D, Cambridge exams policy, etc.).

firestoreSchema._purpose: Pre-declares the Firestore shape for Phase 2 (self-assessment) and Phase 3 (institutional maturity + appraisal integration). Authoritative — the rules block in Central Hub/firestore.rules and the schema doc in docs/architecture/FIRESTORE_SCHEMA.md §23 should both read from here.

ai_competency_self_assessments.scope: Per-teacher annual self-assessment of Part 1 (Teacher AI Competency)

fields.schoolId: partner_schools doc id (denormalised for school-scope rule queries)
fields.selfDeclaredLevel: 'foundation' | 'practitioner' | 'leader' — highest level where majority of domains are 'achieved'

lineManagerValidation.validatedBy: uid of line manager (typically AH school_principal or CH coordinator)
lineManagerValidation.agreedLevel: 'foundation' | 'practitioner' | 'leader' — line manager's agreed level (may differ from selfDeclaredLevel)

ai_competency_self_assessments.ruleSummary: Self-CREATE + self-UPDATE by the teacher (own uid only); line manager validation fields write-restricted to AH school_principal / academic_admin (same school) OR CH coordinator / central_admin. Submitted assessments are read-only to all parties except the validator (who may update validation fields only). Append-only — historical records preserved for trend analysis.

domainRatings._shape: domainRatings[domain] = { currentLevel, evidence[], strengths[], improvementAreas[], targetLevel }
domainRatings._example: domainRatings.strategy_leadership = { currentLevel: 3, evidence: ['AI strategy doc adopted 2026-01', ...], strengths: [...], improvementAreas: [...], targetLevel: 4 }

appraisalValidation.appraiserUid: uid of Eduversal Academic Directorate appraiser (CH director / central_admin)

ai_maturity_assessments.ruleSummary: CREATE + UPDATE by AH school_principal / academic_admin (own school) OR CH central_admin. Appraisal fields write-restricted to CH director / central_admin. Read by same-school AH leadership + all CH director / coordinator / admin (network-wide reporting). Immutable on appraiser-validation. Aggregated annually by Cloud Function for network-level reporting.

ai_competency_aggregates.scope: Cloud-Function-maintained aggregates of self-assessments + maturity assessments
ai_competency_aggregates.purpose: Power network-level AI integration reports and school benchmarking dashboards without on-the-fly aggregation
ai_competency_aggregates.writeAccess: Cloud Function only (mirrors school_leaderboards pattern, Common Mistake #44)

pageAccess._note: Page-access slugs to be seeded for the 3 reader pages + 2 admin pages. Defaults open-to-all (visible_to: []) for reader pages; admin pages restricted.

implementationPlan._note: Mirrors the TodoWrite list. Single source of truth for what's done, what's in flight, what's next.

phase1a.blocker: After this manifest is approved, practical layer authoring begins; reference verbatim JSONs follow practical drafts.
